item: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.
notes: Agent A begins by clearly agreeing that society's interests should take priority, then shifts toward Agent B's rights-protection and balance frame by turns 3 and 5. Agent B starts by calling the statement too rigid, then also moves toward a balance frame in the final turn. Both agents converge near neutral/balance. Agent B turn 4 contains non-Latin script inside an English sentence; scores are based on observable argument content.
